what_it_does: This file implements the G5 activation-context substrate for non-turn cognition triggers. It declares a registry of activation contexts (per-turn, background, sense, rollup), provides entry points to fire background/sense casts and consolidate rollup records, adds tickable decision-layer drivers (idle gate, event intake, rollup cursor), and a deterministic file-discovered mode auto-detector that feeds an existing toggle. It enforces mode-allocated slot budgets, a sacred reserve floor for live per-turn calls, and routes outputs only through non-consequential destinations.
observations: ACTIVATION_CONTEXTS is a declared-data registry mapping each context to trigger kind, driver note, reply flag, swarm flag, owner, allowed destinations, and description.
The per-turn context is registered but owned by chat()/chat_parts(), so fire_activation refuses to handle it.
The rollup context is read-half: it fires no swarm and only consolidates existing cognition.wave telemetry into a distribution at a run:// address.
fire_activation fails loud for unknown contexts, contexts not owned by it, contexts not live for the mode, and reserve_r below FLOOR_RESERVE_R.
In no-reply contexts, inject routing decisions are re-landed at run:// addresses rather than silently dropped.
consolidate_rollup uses statistics for median/p95 and reports reclaimable run:// addresses when gc=True but does not delete refs or events.
The H drivers are tickable pure-ish functions, not daemons; the always-on callers are explicitly flagged as needs-tim.
activity_signal reads the last 200 events, computes idle_seconds from OPERATOR_ACTIVITY_KINDS, and reads inbox size via counts.escalations.
Mode detection walks suite.mode_detection_rule_registry.ordered() in priority order, validates the candidate against suite.MODES, and delegates to suite.autodetect_mode.
standouts: The sacred reserve floor FLOOR_RESERVE_R prevents background/sense casts from starving live per-turn slots.
Reuse-don't-parallel: run_swarm, rules.route, surface_review, run_stats dist, and suite.autodetect_mode are all reused instead of building parallel schedulers or executors.
The RollupDriver holds a since cursor so each tick consolidates only new cognition.wave records.
Mode detection is deterministic and registry-driven, not a model or inline if/else ladder.
conventions: Heavy docstring-driven design with law references (C5.5, G9, L1/L2) and drift-home notes.
Dataclass used for the ActivationResult artifact.
Cross-module contracts rely on suite.* and _cog.*/_rules.* APIs assumed to exist elsewhere.
Constants are UPPER_SNAKE and declared near the top of the file.
Functions use Python type annotations and keyword-only optional parameters.
concerns: Many cross-module dependencies are assumed but not visible in this file, making contract verification hard from the file alone.
activity_signal swallows inbox read exceptions and falls back to 0, which could mask wiring errors.
_iso_to_epoch fail-soft returns None on malformed timestamps, and a junk timestamp could be treated as no recent activity.
fire_activation re-lands inject decisions as address writes without checking value serializability or address scheme validity.
consolidate_rollup uses an approximate p95 index (round(0.95*(n-1))) rather than a documented nearest-rank method.
The module mixes substrate entry points, drivers, and mode detection, giving it a broad surface area.
notes: This file is the G5 activation substrate: declared contexts, budget-guarded non-turn cast firing, rollup consolidation, tickable drivers, and a deterministic mode detector, all wired through existing runtime pieces.
questions: What exact schema does suite.activation_allocation return, and what are the defaults for missing keys?
How does _cog.SlotBudget.from_registry behave when main_ctx_tokens is 0 or omitted?
What is the precise contract for suite.mode_detection_rule_registry.ordered() and rule.matches(sig)?
Are there tests covering sense_event shaping and raw_event intake edge cases?
How is the run:// address scheme validated elsewhere, and what happens if set_ref receives a non-string cas?
What is the intended cadence and ownership for the needs-tim always-on callers?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it builds the activation-context registry, entry points, mode allocation/budget enforcement, tickable drivers, and deterministic mode detector while explicitly leaving always-on daemons undriven (needs-tim).
apparent_themes: Reuse existing runtime primitives rather than duplicate them
Sacred reserve for the live per-turn stream
Fail-loud validation for contexts, modes, budgets, and rules
Tickable decision layer separated from always-on lifecycle/GPU concerns
Honest GC: report reclaimable addresses without deleting append-only events
intention_signals: The file appears to extend the existing per-turn cognition pipeline with a small, controlled set of non-turn activation contexts (background idle ticks, sense event hooks, timer rollups) and a deterministic mode detector, while keeping the core engine unchanged and avoiding always-on GPU daemons.
novelty: A formal registry of activation contexts that distinguishes trigger kind, reply production, swarm firing, and allowed destinations; a rollup read-half that consolidates the system's own telemetry into a distribution at a run:// address; and a deterministic, file-discovered mode-detection rule registry evaluated as data-ASTs rather than a model or inline ladder.
contribution: This file contributes a structured, budget-guarded activation layer that lets the system fire casts and consolidate telemetry outside user turns, plus tickable driver hooks and a deterministic mode detector, all designed to reuse existing swarm/rule/runtime machinery rather than duplicate it.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/activation.py declares activation contexts, fires budget-reserved background and sense casts, consolidates cognition telemetry into run:// rollup records, and provides tickable drivers plus a deterministic mode detector for a concurrent cognition runtime.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to generalize the presence dial into named non-turn activation contexts and a mode detector while reusing the existing per-turn cognition spine and avoiding always-on GPU-consuming daemons.

symbols.does: Dataclass that captures the artifact of a fired activation context.
symbols.description: Holds context, mode, turn_id, trigger, reply flag, fired roles, routed outputs, slot budget snapshot, optional rollup/address, and wall-clock duration for inspection after an activation.

symbols.does: Collects buildable G3 routing rules from a cast.
symbols.description: Returns the canonical injection rule plus any AST-shaped rules declared by fireable roles, using the Suite's rule builder; descriptive role rule entries are skipped.

symbols.does: Entry point that fires a non-turn background or sense cast under a mode's budget.
symbols.description: Validates the context, mode allocation, and reserve floor; builds a slot budget, shapes an utterance, runs the cast via run_swarm, and routes outputs through rules.route over the allowed non-consequential destinations, re-landing inject decisions as run:// addresses in no-reply contexts.

symbols.does: Entry point that consolidates the swarm's own run-records into a rollup at a run:// address.
symbols.description: Reads cognition.wave events since a given seq, aggregates per-role latency into distributions, persists the rollup via the content-addressed store, and optionally reports reclaimable run:// addresses without deleting refs or events.

symbols.does: Local helper that builds a small distribution summary from a list of values.
symbols.description: Computes n, median, approximate p95, min, and max for a list of numeric latency samples, returning None for an empty list.

symbols.does: Generates a turn id string for an activation context.
symbols.description: Builds a unique id prefixed with the context name using the current UTC date/time and a millisecond counter.

symbols.does: Parses an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp to epoch seconds.
symbols.description: Converts an event ts string to a float, returning None on missing or malformed input so the idle gate fails soft on a single bad timestamp.

symbols.does: Reads the shared event log and produces a deterministic activity snapshot.
symbols.description: Computes seconds since the last operator-activity event, the last activity kind, current mode, inbox escalation count, and recent event kinds over a bounded window; used by the background driver and mode detector.

symbols.does: Idle-gate driver that decides whether to fire a background cast.
symbols.description: Checks that the mode allocates the background context and that the operator has been quiet for at least idle_seconds, then delegates to fire_activation('background') and returns a legible fired/reason record.

symbols.does: Event-intake driver that fires a sense cast from a raw event.
symbols.description: Validates that the raw event is a dict, shapes it into a sense_event with a summary, checks the mode allocates the sense context, and delegates to fire_activation('sense').

symbols.does: Stateful driver that holds a rollup cursor across timer ticks.
symbols.description: Maintains a since seq cursor so each tick consolidates only new cognition.wave records, advancing the cursor to the log head and returning legible consolidated/no-op results.

symbols.does: Performs one timer tick of rollup consolidation.
symbols.description: Checks for new cognition.wave records since the held cursor, calls consolidate_rollup when present, advances the cursor to the current log head, and reports before/after cursor positions.

symbols.does: Returns the current event log head seq.
symbols.description: Reads all events and returns the seq of the last event, or -1 if the log is empty so the next tick starts from the beginning.

symbols.does: Deterministically produces a candidate mode from the live activity signal.
symbols.description: Walks the priority-ordered mode-detection rule registry, evaluates each rule's data-AST condition against the activity signal, validates the candidate against suite.MODES, and returns the first match or None.

symbols.does: Feeds a detected mode candidate to the existing autodetect toggle.
symbols.description: Runs the detector and, when a new candidate differs from the live mode, calls suite.autodetect_mode so the off/suggest/auto toggle decides whether to ignore, surface, or apply the change.
